Now Nahash, king of the Ammonites, had been grievously oppressing the Gadites and the Reubenites. He would gouge out the right eye of each of them and would not grant Israel a deliverer. No one was left of the Israelites across the Jordan whose right eye Nahash, king of the Ammonites, had not gouged out. But there were seven thousand men who had escaped from the Ammonites and had entered Jabesh-gilead.
text.Rotherham: But, abandoned men, said—How can this one save us? So they treated him with contempt, and brought him no present,—but he was as one that was deaf.
text.KJV: 10:27 But the children of Belial sayd, Howe shall this man saue vs? and they despised him, and brought him no presents: but he held his peace.
text.DRV: 10:27 And he made that there was as great aboundance of silver in Jerusalem, as of stones: and of cedar trees he caused such a multitude, as if it were sycamore trees, which grow in the plains.

text.NRSV: About a month later, Nahash the Ammonite went up and besieged Jabesh-gilead; and all the men of Jabesh said to Nahash, “Make a treaty with us, and we will serve you.”
text.Rotherham: Then came up Nahash the Ammonite, and encamped against Jabesh-gilead,—and all the man of Jabesh said unto Nahash, Solemnise with us a covenant, that we may serve thee.
text.KJV: 11:1 Then Nahash the Ammonite came vp, and encamped against Iabesh Gilead: and all the men of Iabesh sayde vnto Nahash, Make a couenant with vs, and we will serue thee.
text.DRV: 11:1 And king Salomon loved many women strangers, the daughter also of Pharao, and Moabites, & Ammonites, Idumeians, and Sidonians, & Hetheians:

text.NRSV: But Nahash the Ammonite said to them, “On this condition I will make a treaty with you, namely that I gouge out everyone’s right eye, and thus put disgrace upon all Israel.”
text.Rotherham: And Nahash the Ammonite said unto them, Hereby, will I solemnise [a covenant] with you by digging out for you, every one’s right eye,—and laying it for a reproach, upon all Israel.
text.KJV: 11:2 And Nahash the Ammonite answered them, On this condition will I make a couenant with you, that I may thrust out all your right eyes, and lay it for a reproch vpon all Israel.
text.DRV: 11:2 of the nations, whereof our Lord said to the children of Israel: You shall not go in unto them, neither shall any of them come in unto yours: for they will most certainly turn away your hearts to follow their gods. To these therefore was Salomon coupled in most fervent love.

text.NRSV: The elders of Jabesh said to him, “Give us seven days’ respite that we may send messengers through all the territory of Israel. Then, if there is no one to save us, we will give ourselves up to you.”
text.Rotherham: And the elders of Jabesh said unto him—Give us a respite of seven days, that we may send messengers throughout all the bounds of Israel,—and then, if there is none to save us, we will come out unto thee.
text.KJV: 11:3 And the Elders of Iabesh sayd vnto him, Giue vs seuen daies respite, that we may send messengers vnto all the coasts of Israel: and then, if there be no man to saue vs, we will come out to thee.
text.DRV: 11:3 And he had wives as it were queens seven hundred, and concubines three hundred: and the women turned away his heart.

text.NRSV: When the messengers came to Gibeah of Saul, they reported the matter in the hearing of the people; and all the people wept aloud.
text.Rotherham: And, when the messengers came to Gibeah of Saul, and spake the words in the ears of the people, all the people lifted up their voice, and wept.
text.KJV: 11:4 ¶ Then came the messengers to Gibeah of Saul, and told the tidings in the eares of the people: and all the people lift vp their voyces, and wept.
text.DRV: 11:4 And when he was now old, his heart was depraved by women, that he followed strange gods: neither was his heart perfect with our Lord his God, as the heart of David his father.

text.NRSV: Now Saul was coming from the field behind the oxen; and Saul said, “What is the matter with the people, that they are weeping?” So they told him the message from the inhabitants of Jabesh.
text.Rotherham: But lo! Saul, came in, following the oxen, out of the field, and Saul said, What aileth the people, that they should weep? Then were recounted to him the words of the men of Jabesh.
text.KJV: 11:5 And behold, Saul came after the herd out of the field, and Saul sayd, What aileth the people that they weep? and they told him the tidings of the men of Iabesh.
text.DRV: 11:5 But Salomon worshipped Astarthee the goddess of the Sidonians, and Moloch the idol of the Ammonites.

text.NRSV: And the spirit of God came upon Saul in power when he heard these words, and his anger was greatly kindled.
text.Rotherham: And the Spirit of God came suddenly upon Saul, when he heard these words,—and his anger raged furiously.
text.KJV: 11:6 And the Spirit of God came vpon Saul, when he heard those tydings, and his anger was kindled greatly.
text.DRV: 11:6 And Salomon did that which was not liked before our Lord, and he accomplished not to follow our Lord, as David his father.

text.NRSV: He took a yoke of oxen, and cut them in pieces and sent them throughout all the territory of Israel by messengers, saying, “Whoever does not come out after Saul and Samuel, so shall it be done to his oxen!” Then the dread of the Lord fell upon the people, and they came out as one.
text.Rotherham: So he took a yoke of oxen, and cut them in pieces, and sent throughout all the bounds of Israel by the hand of messengers—saying, Whosoever cometh not forth after Saul, and after Samuel, so, shall it be done unto his oxen. Then felt the dread of Yahweh, upon the people, and they came forth, as one man.
text.KJV: 11:7 And he tooke a yoke of oxen, and hewed them in pieces, and sent them throughout all the coasts of Israel by the hands of messengers, saying, Whosoeuer commeth not foorth after Saul and after Samuel, so shall it bee done vnto his oxen: and the feare of the Lord fell on the people, and they came out with one consent.
text.DRV: 11:7 Then built Salomon a temple to Chamos the idol of Moab, in the mount that is against Jerusalem, and to Moloch the idol of the children of Ammon.

text.NRSV: When he mustered them at Bezek, those from Israel were three hundred thousand, and those from Judah seventy thousand.
text.Rotherham: And, when he numbered them in Bezek, the sons of Israel were found to be—three hundred thousand, and the men of Judah thirty thousand.
text.KJV: 11:8 And when he numbred them in Bezek, the children of Israel were three hundred thousand, and the men of Iudah thirty thousand.
text.DRV: 11:8 And in this manner did he to all his wives that were strangers, which burnt frankincense, & immolated to their gods.

text.NRSV: They said to the messengers who had come, “Thus shall you say to the inhabitants of Jabesh-gilead: ‘Tomorrow, by the time the sun is hot, you shall have deliverance.’” When the messengers came and told the inhabitants of Jabesh, they rejoiced.
text.Rotherham: So they said unto the messengers who had come—Thus, shall ye surely say to the men of Jabesh-gilead, To-morrow, shall ye have deliverance, about the time the son is hot. And, when the messengers came and told the men of Jabesh, they rejoiced.
text.KJV: 11:9 And they said vnto the messengers that came, Thus shall yee say vnto the men of Iabesh Gilead, To morrow by that time the sunne be hote, ye shal haue helpe. And the messengers came, and shewed it to the men of Iabesh, and they were glad.
text.DRV: 11:9 Therefore our Lord was wrath with Salomon, because his mind was turned away from our Lord the God of Israel, who had appeared unto him the second time,

text.NRSV: So the inhabitants of Jabesh said, “Tomorrow we will give ourselves up to you, and you may do to us whatever seems good to you.”
text.Rotherham: So the men of Jabesh said, To-morrow, will we come out unto you,—Then shall ye do with us, according to all that is good in your eyes.
text.KJV: 11:10 Therfore the men of Iabesh said, To morrow wee will come out vnto you, and ye shall doe with vs all that seemeth good vnto you.
text.DRV: 11:10 and had commanded him concerning this word, that he should not follow strange gods, & he kept not the things which our Lord commanded him.

text.NRSV: The next day Saul put the people in three companies. At the morning watch they came into the camp and cut down the Ammonites until the heat of the day; and those who survived were scattered, so that no two of them were left together.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass on the morrow, that Saul set the people in three companies, and they entered into the midst of the host, during the morning watch, and smote Ammon until the day was hot. And it came to pass that, they who were left, were scattered, so that there were not left among them, two together.
text.KJV: 11:11 And it was so on the morrow, that Saul put the people in three cōpanies, and they came into the midst of the host in the morning watch, and slewe the Ammonites, vntill the heat of the day: and it came to passe, that they which remained were scattered, so that two of them were not left together.
text.DRV: 11:11 Our Lord therefore said to Salomon: Because thou hast done this, and hast not kept my covenant, and my precepts, which I have commanded thee, breaking I will rent asunder thy Kingdom, and will give it to thy servant.

text.NRSV: The people said to Samuel, “Who is it that said, ‘Shall Saul reign over us?’ Give them to us so that we may put them to death.”
text.Rotherham: Then said the people unto Samuel, Who is he that was saying, Shall, Saul, reign over us? Give up the men, that we may put them to death.
text.KJV: 11:12 ¶ And the people said vnto Samuel, Who is he that said, Shall Saul reigne ouer vs? bring the men, that we may put them to death.
text.DRV: 11:12 Nevertheless in thy days I will not do it because of David thy father: out of the hand of thy son I will rent it,

text.NRSV: But Saul said, “No one shall be put to death this day, for today the Lord has brought deliverance to Israel.”
text.Rotherham: But Saul said, There shall not be put to death a man, this day,—for, to-day, hath Yahweh wrought deliverance in Israel.
text.KJV: 11:13 And Saul said, There shall not a man be put to death this day: for to day the Lord hath wrought saluation in Israel.
text.DRV: 11:13 neither will I take away the whole Kingdom, but one tribe I will give to thy son for David my servant, and Jerusalem, which I have chosen.

text.NRSV: Samuel said to the people, “Come, let us go to Gilgal and there renew the kingship.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Samuel unto the people, Come and let us go to Gilgal,—and let us there renew the kingdom.
text.KJV: 11:14 Then said Samuel to the people, Come, and let vs goe to Gilgal, and renew the kingdome there.
text.DRV: 11:14 And our Lord raised up an adversary to Salomon, Adad an Idumeite of the king's seed, who was in Edom.

text.NRSV: So all the people went to Gilgal, and there they made Saul king before the Lord in Gilgal. There they sacrificed offerings of well-being before the Lord, and there Saul and all the Israelites rejoiced greatly.
text.Rotherham: So all the people went to Gilgal, and made Saul king there, before Yahweh, in Gilgal, and offered there sacrifices of peace-offerings before Yahweh,—and Saul and all the men of Israel rejoiced there, exceedingly.
text.KJV: 11:15 And all the people went to Gilgal, and there they made Saul King before the Lord in Gilgal: and there they sacrificed sacrifices of peace offerings before the Lord: and there Saul and all the men of Israel reioyced greatly.
text.DRV: 11:15 For when David was in Idumea, and Joab the general of the warfare was gone up to bury them that were slain, and had slain all male kind in Idumea,

text.NRSV: Samuel said to all Israel, “I have listened to you in all that you have said to me, and have set a king over you.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel said unto all Israel, Lo! I have hearkened unto your voice, in all that ye said to me,—and have set over you a king.
text.KJV: 12:1 And Samuel saide vnto all Israel, Beholde, I haue hearkned vnto your voice in all that ye said vnto mee, and haue made a King ouer you.
text.DRV: 12:1 And Roboam came into Sichem: for thither was all Israel gathered together to make him king.

text.NRSV: See, it is the king who leads you now; I am old and gray, but my sons are with you. I have led you from my youth until this day.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, lo! the king going to and fro before you, But, I, am old and grey-headed, and, my sons, lo! they are with you,—But, I, have gone to and fro before you from my youth until this day.
text.KJV: 12:2 And now behold, the King walketh before you: and I am olde, and gray headed, and behold, my sonnes are with you: and I haue walked before you from my childhood vnto this day.
text.DRV: 12:2 But Jeroboam the son of Nabat, when he was yet in Ægypt fugitive from the face of king Salomon, hearing of his death; returned out of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: Here I am; testify against me before the Lord and before his anointed. Whose ox have I taken? Or whose donkey have I taken? Or whom have I defrauded? Whom have I oppressed? Or from whose hand have I taken a bribe to blind my eyes with it? Testify against me and I will restore it to you.”
text.Rotherham: Behold me! testify against me, before Yahweh, and before his Anointed—Whose, ox, have I taken? or whose, ass, have I taken? or whom have I oppressed? Whom have I crushed? or at whose, hands, have I taken a bribe, to cover up mine eyes therewith? and I will restore it unto you.
text.KJV: 12:3 Behold, here I am, witnesse against me before the Lord, and before his Anoynted: Whose oxe haue I taken? or whose asse haue I taken? or whom haue I defrauded? whom haue I oppressed? or of whose hand haue I receiued any bribe to blinde mine eyes therewith? and I will restore it you.
text.DRV: 12:3 And they sent and called him: Jeroboam therefore came, and all the multitude of Israel, and they spake to Roboam, saying.

text.NRSV: They said, “You have not defrauded us or oppressed us or taken anything from the hand of anyone.”
text.Rotherham: And they said, Thou hast not oppressed us, neither hast thou crushed us,—neither hast thou taken, at the hand of any man, any thing.
text.KJV: 12:4 And they said, Thou hast not defrauded vs, nor oppressed vs, neither hast thou taken ought of any mans hand.
text.DRV: 12:4 Thy father laid a most hard yoke upon us: thou therefore diminish now a little of thy father's most hard empire, and of the most heavy yoke, that he laid upon us, and we will serve thee.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “The Lord is witness against you, and his anointed is witness this day, that you have not found anything in my hand.” And they said, “He is witness.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—Witness, is Yahweh against you, and, witness, is his Anointed, this day, that ye have not found in my hand, any thing! And they said: Witness!
text.KJV: 12:5 And hee said vnto them, The Lord is witnesse against you, and his Anointed is witnesse this day, that ye haue not found ought in my hand: And they answered, He is witnesse.
text.DRV: 12:5 Who said to them: Go until the third day, and return to me. And when the people was gone,

text.NRSV: Samuel said to the people, “The Lord is witness, who appointed Moses and Aaron and brought your ancestors up out of the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel said unto the people: Witness, is Yahweh, who wrought with Moses and with Aaron, and who brought up your fathers out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: 12:6 ¶ And Samuel said vnto the people, It is the Lord that aduanced Moses and Aaron, and that brought your fathers vp out of the land of Egypt.
text.DRV: 12:6 King Roboam took counsel with the ancients, that assisted before Salomon his father, whiles he yet lived, and he said: What counsel do you give me, that I may answer this people?

text.NRSV: Now therefore take your stand, so that I may enter into judgment with you before the Lord, and I will declare to you all the saving deeds of the Lord that he performed for you and for your ancestors.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, take your stand and let me plead with you, before Yahweh,—and tell you all the righteous acts of Yahweh, which he wrought with you, and with your fathers:
text.KJV: 12:7 Now therefore stand still, that I may reason with you before the Lord, of all the righteous acts of the Lord, which he did to you and your fathers.
text.DRV: 12:7 Who said to him: If this day thou wilt yield to this people, and condescend to them, and grant to their petition, & wilt speak to them gentle words, they will be thy servants always.

text.NRSV: When Jacob went into Egypt and the Egyptians oppressed them, then your ancestors cried to the Lord and the Lord sent Moses and Aaron, who brought forth your ancestors out of Egypt, and settled them in this place.
text.Rotherham: How that, when Jacob had come into Egypt,—and your fathers had made outcry unto Yahweh, then Yahweh sent Moses and Aaron, and they brought forth your fathers out of Egypt, and he caused them to dwell in this place;
text.KJV: 12:8 When Iacob was come into Egypt, and your fathers cried vnto the Lord, then the Lord sent Moses and Aaron, which brought foorth your fathers out of Egypt, and made them dwell in this place.
text.DRV: 12:8 Who left the counsel of the ancients, which they had given him, and admitted youngmen, that had been brought up with him, & waited on him,

text.NRSV: But they forgot the Lord their God; and he sold them into the hand of Sisera, commander of the army of King Jabin of Hazor, and into the hand of the Philistines, and into the hand of the king of Moab; and they fought against them.
text.Rotherham: And, when they forgat Yahweh their God, he sold them into the hand of Sisera, prince of the host of Jabin, king of Hazor, and into the hand of the Philistines, and into the hand of the king of Moab, and they fought against them;
text.KJV: 12:9 And when they forgat the Lord their God, he sold them into the hand of Sisera captaine of the hoste of Hazor, and into the hand of the Philistines, and into the hand of the king of Moab, and they fought against them.
text.DRV: 12:9 and he said to them: What counsel give you me, that I may answer this people, which have said to me: Make the yoke lighter which thy father hath put upon us?

text.NRSV: Then they cried to the Lord, and said, ‘We have sinned, because we have forsaken the Lord, and have served the Baals and the Astartes; but now rescue us out of the hand of our enemies, and we will serve you.’
text.Rotherham: But, when they made outcry unto Yahweh and said—We have sinned, in that we have forsaken Yahweh, and have served the Baals and the Ashtoreths,—now, therefore, deliver us out of the hand of our enemies, that we may serve thee,
text.KJV: 12:10 And they cried vnto the Lord, and said, Wee haue sinned, because we haue forsaken the Lord, and haue serued Baalim and Ashtaroth: but now deliuer vs out of the hand of our enemies, and we will serue thee.
text.DRV: 12:10 And the youngmen that had been brought up with him, said: Thus speak to this people, which have spoken to thee, saying: Thy father aggravated our yoke, do thou ease it. Thus shalt thou speak to them: My least finger is grosser than the back of my father.

text.NRSV: And the Lord sent Jerubbaal and Barak, and Jephthah, and Samson, and rescued you out of the hand of your enemies on every side; and you lived in safety.
text.Rotherham: then Yahweh sent Jerubbaal and Bedan, and Jephthah, and Samuel,—and delivered you out of the hand of your enemies, on every side, and ye dwelt in safety.
text.KJV: 12:11 And the Lord sent Ierubbaal, and Bedan, and Iephthah, and Samuel, and deliuered you out of the hand of your enemies on euery side, and yee dwelled safe.
text.DRV: 12:11 And now my father laid upon you a heavy yoke, but I will add upon your yoke: my father beat you with scourges, but I will beat you with scorpions.

text.NRSV: But when you saw that King Nahash of the Ammonites came against you, you said to me, ‘No, but a king shall reign over us,’ though the Lord your God was your king.
text.Rotherham: But, when ye saw that, Nahash king of the sons of Ammon, came upon you, then said ye unto me, Nay! but, a king, shall reign over us, when, Yahweh your God, was your king!
text.KJV: 12:12 And when ye saw that Nahash the king of the children of Ammon came against you, ye said vnto me, Nay, but a King shall reigne ouer vs, when the Lord your God was your King.
text.DRV: 12:12 Jeroboam therefore came, and all the people to Roboam the third day, as the king had spoken, saying: Return to me the third day.

text.NRSV: See, here is the king whom you have chosen, for whom you have asked; see, the Lord has set a king over you.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, lo! the king whom ye have chosen, for whom ye have asked,—lo! therefore, Yahweh hath set over you a king.
text.KJV: 12:13 Now therefore, behold the King whom yee haue chosen, and whom yee haue desired: and behold, the Lord hath set a King ouer you.
text.DRV: 12:13 And the king answered the people rough words, leaving the counsel of the ancients, which they had given him,

text.NRSV: If you will fear the Lord and serve him and heed his voice and not rebel against the commandment of the Lord, and if both you and the king who reigns over you will follow the Lord your God, it will be well;
text.Rotherham: If ye will revere Yahweh, and serve him, and hearken unto his voice, and not rebel against the bidding of Yahweh, then shall, both ye and your king that reigneth over you, continue to follow after Yahweh your God.
text.KJV: 12:14 If ye will feare the Lord, and serue him, and obey his voice, and not rebell against the Commandement of the Lord, then shall both ye and also the King that reigneth ouer you, continue following the Lord your God.
text.DRV: 12:14 and he spake to them according to the counsel of the youngmen, saying: My father made your yoke heavy, but I will add to your yoke: my father beat you with whips, but I will beat you with scorpions.

text.NRSV: but if you will not heed the voice of the Lord, but rebel against the commandment of the Lord, then the hand of the Lord will be against you and your king.
text.Rotherham: But, if ye hearken not unto the voice of Yahweh, but rebel against the bidding of Yahweh, then will the hand of Yahweh continue to be against you, and against your fathers.
text.KJV: 12:15 But if ye wil not obey the voice of the Lord, but rebel against the Commandement of the Lord, then shall the hand of the Lord be against you, as it was against your fathers.
text.DRV: 12:15 And the king condescended not to the people: because our Lord was turned away from him, that he might raise up his word, which he had spoken in the hand of Ahias the Silonite, to Jeroboam the son of Nabat.

text.NRSV: Now therefore take your stand and see this great thing that the Lord will do before your eyes.
text.Rotherham: Even now, stand still and see this great thing,—which Yahweh is about to do before your eyes:
text.KJV: 12:16 ¶ Now therefore stand and see this great thing which the Lord will doe before your eyes.
text.DRV: 12:16 The people therefore seeing that the king would not hear them, answered him, saying: What part have we in David? Or what inheritance in the son of Isai? Go into thy tabernacles Israel, now see to thy house David. And Israel went into their tabernacles.

text.NRSV: Is it not the wheat harvest today? I will call upon the Lord, that he may send thunder and rain; and you shall know and see that the wickedness that you have done in the sight of the Lord is great in demanding a king for yourselves.”
text.Rotherham: Is it not wheat harvest, to-day? I will cry unto Yahweh, that he may give forth thunderings and rain,—know ye then and see, that, your wrong, is great which ye have done in the sight of Yahweh, in asking for yourselves, a king.
text.KJV: 12:17 Is it not wheat haruest to day? I will call vnto the Lord, and hee shall send thunder and raine, that ye may perceiue and see that your wickednesse is great, which ye haue done in the sight of the Lord, in asking you a King.
text.DRV: 12:17 But over the children of Israel, whosoever dwelt in the cities of Juda, Roboam reigned.

text.NRSV: So Samuel called upon the Lord, and the Lord sent thunder and rain that day; and all the people greatly feared the Lord and Samuel.
text.Rotherham: So Samuel cried unto Yahweh, and Yahweh gave forth thunderings and rain, on that day,—and all the people greatly feared Yahweh, and Samuel.
text.KJV: 12:18 So Samuel called vnto the Lord, and the Lord sent thunder and raine that day: and all the people greatly feared the Lord and Samuel.
text.DRV: 12:18 King Roboam therefore sent Aduram, who was over the tributes: and all Israel stoned him, and he died, moreover king Roboam in haste went up into his chariot, and fled into Jerusalem:

text.NRSV: All the people said to Samuel, “Pray to the Lord your God for your servants, so that we may not die; for we have added to all our sins the evil of demanding a king for ourselves.”
text.Rotherham: And all the people said unto Samuel—Pray for thy servants, unto Yahweh thy God, and let it not be that we die,—For we have added, to all our sins, a wrong, in asking for ourselves a king.
text.KJV: 12:19 And all the people said vnto Samuel, Pray for thy seruants vnto the Lord thy God, that we die not: for we haue added vnto all our sinnes, this euil, to aske vs a King.
text.DRV: 12:19 and Israel revolted from the house of David, until this present day.

text.NRSV: And Samuel said to the people, “Do not be afraid; you have done all this evil, yet do not turn aside from following the Lord, but serve the Lord with all your heart;
text.Rotherham: Then said Samuel unto the people—Do not fear, ye, have done all this wrong,—nevertheless, do not turn aside from following Yahweh, but serve Yahweh, with all your heart;
text.KJV: 12:20 ¶ And Samuel saide vnto the people, Feare not: (ye haue done al this wickednesse, yet turne not aside from following the Lord, but serue the Lord with all your heart:
text.DRV: 12:20 And it came to pass when all Israel had heard, that Jeroboam was returned, they sent, and called him, an assembly being gathered, and they made him king over all Israel, neither did any man follow the house of David beside the tribe of Juda only.

text.NRSV: and do not turn aside after useless things that cannot profit or save, for they are useless.
text.Rotherham: and turn not aside after vanities, that can neither profit nor deliver, because, vanities, they are.
text.KJV: 12:21 And turne ye not aside, for then should ye goe after vaine things, which cannot profit, nor deliuer, for they are vaine.)
text.DRV: 12:21 And Roboam came to Jerusalem, and gathered together all the house of Juda, and the tribe of Benjamin, an hundred fourscore thousand chosen men warriors, to fight against the house of Israel, and to reduce the kingdom to Roboam the son of Salomon.

text.NRSV: For the Lord will not cast away his people, for his great name’s sake, because it has pleased the Lord to make you a people for himself.
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh will not give up his people, because of his great name, for Yahweh was minded to make you his people.
text.KJV: 12:22 For the Lord wil not forsake his people, for his great Names sake: because it hath pleased the Lord to make you his people.
text.DRV: 12:22 But the word of our Lord came to Semeias the man of God, saying:

text.NRSV: Moreover as for me, far be it from me that I should sin against the Lord by ceasing to pray for you; and I will instruct you in the good and the right way.
text.Rotherham: As for me also, far be it from me, that I should sin against Yahweh, by ceasing to pray for you,—but I will direct you, in the good and right way.
text.KJV: 12:23 Moreouer, as for me, God forbid that I should sin against the Lord, in ceasing to pray for you: but I will teach you the good and the right way.
text.DRV: 12:23 Speak to Roboam the son of Salomon, the king of Juda, and to all the house of Juda, and Benjamin, and the rest of the people, saying:

text.NRSV: Only fear the Lord, and serve him faithfully with all your heart; for consider what great things he has done for you.
text.Rotherham: Only, revere Yahweh, and serve him in truth, with all your heart,—for see, what great things he hath done with you.
text.KJV: 12:24 Onely feare the Lord, and serue him in trueth with all your heart: for consider how great things he hath done for you.
text.DRV: 12:24 Thus saith our Lord: You shall not go up, neither shall you fight against your brethren the children of Israel: let every man return into his house, for this word is done by me. They heard the word of our Lord, & returned from their journey as our Lord had commanded them.

text.NRSV: But if you still do wickedly, you shall be swept away, both you and your king.”
text.Rotherham: But, if ye, will do wrong, both ye and your king shall be swept away.
text.KJV: 12:25 But if yee shall still doe wickedly, yee shall be consumed, both yee and your King.
text.DRV: 12:25 And Jeroboam built Sichem in mount Ephraim, and dwelt there: and departing thence he built Phanuel.

text.NRSV: Saul was thirty years old when he began to reign, and he reigned forty-two years over Israel.
text.Rotherham: Saul was [thirty] years old when he began to reign; and, when he had reigned two years over Israel,
text.KJV: 13:1 Saul reigned one yeere, and when he had reigned two yeeres ouer Israel,
text.DRV: 13:1 And behold a man of God came out of Juda, in the word of our Lord into Bethel, Jeroboam standing upon the altar, and censing.

text.NRSV: Saul chose three thousand out of Israel; two thousand were with Saul in Michmash and the hill country of Bethel, and a thousand were with Jonathan in Gibeah of Benjamin; the rest of the people he sent home to their tents.
text.Rotherham: Saul chose him three thousand men out of Israel, of whom there were with Saul, two thousand in Michmash and in the hill-country of Bethel, and, a thousand, were with Jonathan, in Gibeah of Benjamin,—but, the rest of the people, he let go, every man to his own home.
text.KJV: 13:2 Saul chose him three thousand men of Israel: whereof two thousand were with Saul in Michmash, and in mount Bethel, and a thousand were with Ionathan in Gibeah of Beniamin: and the rest of the people he sent euery man to his tent.
text.DRV: 13:2 And he cried out against the altar in the word of our Lord, and said: Altar, altar, thus saith our Lord: Behold a child shall be born to the house of David, named Josias, and he shall immolate upon thee priests of the excelses, which now do burn frankincense on thee, and he shall burn men's bones upon thee.

text.NRSV: Jonathan defeated the garrison of the Philistines that was at Geba; and the Philistines heard of it. And Saul blew the trumpet throughout all the land, saying, “Let the Hebrews hear!”
text.Rotherham: Then did Jonathan smite the garrison of Philistines that was in Geba, and the Philistines heard of it,—and, Saul, blew with a horn throughout all the land saying, Let the Hebrews hear!
text.KJV: 13:3 And Ionathan smote the garison of the Philistines that was in Geba, and the Philistines heard of it: and Saul blew the trumpet thorowout all the land, saying, Let the Hebrewes heare.
text.DRV: 13:3 And he gave a sign in that day, saying: This shall be the sign, that our Lord hath spoken: Behold the altar shall be cloven, and the ashes on it shall be poured out.

text.NRSV: When all Israel heard that Saul had defeated the garrison of the Philistines, and also that Israel had become odious to the Philistines, the people were called out to join Saul at Gilgal.
text.Rotherham: And, all Israel, heard say—Saul hath smitten the garrison of the Philistines, Moreover, also, Israel have made themselves odious among the Philistines,—so the people were called together to follow Saul, to Gilgal.
text.KJV: 13:4 And all Israel heard say, that Saul had smitten a garison of the Philistines, and that Israel also was had in abomination with the Philistines: and the people were called together after Saul to Gilgal.
text.DRV: 13:4 And when the king had heard the word of the man of God, which he cried out against the altar in Bethel, he stretched forth his hand from the altar, saying: Take him. And his hand withered, which he stretched forth against him: neither was he able to draw it back upon him.

text.NRSV: The Philistines mustered to fight with Israel, thirty thousand chariots, and six thousand horsemen, and troops like the sand on the seashore in multitude; they came up and encamped at Michmash, to the east of Beth-aven.
text.Rotherham: And, the Philistines, gathered themselves together to fight with Israel, thirty thousand chariots, and six thousand horsemen, a people also like the sand that is on the sea-shore for multitude,—and they came up, and encamped in Michmash, east of Beth-aven.
text.KJV: 13:5 ¶ And the Philistines gathered themselues together, to fight with Israel, thirtie thousand charets, and sixe thousand horsemen, and people as the sand which is on the sea shore in multitude, and they came vp, and pitched in Michmash, Eastward from Bethauen.
text.DRV: 13:5 The altar also was cloven, and the ashes were poured out of the altar, according to the sign which the man of God had told before in the word of our Lord.

text.NRSV: When the Israelites saw that they were in distress (for the troops were hard pressed), the people hid themselves in caves and in holes and in rocks and in tombs and in cisterns.
text.Rotherham: So, the men of Israel, saw they were in a strait, for the people had been harassed,—and the people had hidden themselves in caves, and in thickets, and among cliffs, and in holes, and in pits.
text.KJV: 13:6 When the men of Israel saw that they were in a strait: (for the people were distressed:) then the people did hide themselues in caues, and in thickets, and in rocks, and in high places, and in pits.
text.DRV: 13:6 And the king said to the man of God: Beseech the face of our Lord thy God, and pray for me, that my hand may be restored me. And the man of God besought the face of our Lord, and the king's hand was restored to him, and it became as it was before.

text.NRSV: Some Hebrews crossed the Jordan to the land of Gad and Gilead. Saul was still at Gilgal, and all the people followed him trembling.
text.Rotherham: And, Hebrews, had passed over the Jordan, to the land of Gad, and Gilead,—but, Saul, was yet in Gilgal, and all the people, trembled after him.
text.KJV: 13:7 And some of the Hebrewes went ouer Iordane, to the land of Gad and Gilead; as for Saul, he was yet in Gilgal, and all the people followed him trembling.
text.DRV: 13:7 And the king spake to the man of God: Come home with me, that thou mayst dine, and I will give thee gifts.

text.NRSV: He waited seven days, the time appointed by Samuel; but Samuel did not come to Gilgal, and the people began to slip away from Saul.
text.Rotherham: And he waited seven days, by the set time that Samuel had named, but Samuel came not to Gilgal,—and the people were scattered from him.
text.KJV: 13:8 ¶ And he taried seuen dayes, according to the set time that Samuel had appointed: but Samuel came not to Gilgal, and the people were scattered from him.
text.DRV: 13:8 And the man of God answered the king: If thou wouldest give me the half part of thy house, I will not come with thee, nor eat bread, nor drink water in this place:

text.NRSV: So Saul said, “Bring the burnt offering here to me, and the offerings of well-being.” And he offered the burnt offering.
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul, Bring near unto me, the ascending-sacrifice and the peace-offerings. And he offered up the ascending-sacrifice.
text.KJV: 13:9 And Saul said, Bring hither a burnt offring to me, and peace offrings. And he offered the burnt offering.
text.DRV: 13:9 for so was it enjoined in the word of our Lord commanding: Thou shalt not eat bread, nor drink water, nor return by the way that thou camest.

text.NRSV: As soon as he had finished offering the burnt offering, Samuel arrived; and Saul went out to meet him and salute him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as he had made an end of offering up the ascending-sacrifice, lo! Samuel, had come,—and Saul went out to meet him, that he might bless him.
text.KJV: 13:10 And it came to passe that assoone as he had made an end of offering the burnt offering, behold, Samuel came, and Saul went out to meete him, that he might salute him.
text.DRV: 13:10 He departed therefore by another way, and returned not by the way, that he came into Bethel.

text.NRSV: Samuel said, “What have you done?” Saul replied, “When I saw that the people were slipping away from me, and that you did not come within the days appointed, and that the Philistines were mustering at Michmash,
text.Rotherham: Then said Samuel—What hast thou done? And Saul said—Because I saw that the people had been scattered from me, and, thou, hadst not come within the appointed days, and, the Philistines, had gathered themselves together to Michmash,
text.KJV: 13:11 ¶ And Samuel said, What hast thou done? And Saul said, Because I sawe that the people were scattered from me, and that thou camest not within the dayes appointed, and that the Philistines gathered themselues together to Michmash:
text.DRV: 13:11 And a certain Prophet being old dwelt in Bethel, to whom his sons came and told him all the works, that the man of God had done that day in Bethel: and the words which he had spoken to the king, they told their father.

text.NRSV: I said, ‘Now the Philistines will come down upon me at Gilgal, and I have not entreated the favor of the Lord’; so I forced myself, and offered the burnt offering.”
text.Rotherham: therefore I said—Now, will the Philistines come down against me, at Gilgal, but, the face of Yahweh, have I not appeased: So I forced myself, and offered up the ascending-sacrifice.
text.KJV: 13:12 Therfore said I, The Philistines will come downe now vpon me to Gilgal, and I haue not made supplication vnto the Lord: I forced my selfe therefore, and offered a burnt offering.
text.DRV: 13:12 And their father said to them: What way went he? His sons shewed him the way, by which the man of God was gone, which came out of Juda.

text.NRSV: Samuel said to Saul, “You have done foolishly; you have not kept the commandment of the Lord your God, which he commanded you. The Lord would have established your kingdom over Israel forever,
text.Rotherham: And Samuel said unto Saul—Thou hast shewn thyself foolish,—thou hast not kept the commandment of Yahweh thy God, which he commanded thee, for, now, would Yahweh have established thy kingdom unto Israel until times age-abiding;
text.KJV: 13:13 And Samuel said to Saul, Thou hast done foolishly: thou hast not kept the commandement of the Lord thy God, which hee commanded thee: for now would the Lord haue established thy kingdome vpon Israel for euer.
text.DRV: 13:13 And he said to his sons: Saddle me an ass. Who when they had saddled it, he got up,

text.NRSV: but now your kingdom will not continue; the Lord has sought out a man after his own heart; and the Lord has appointed him to be ruler over his people, because you have not kept what the Lord commanded you.”
text.Rotherham: Whereas, now, shall thy kingdom not be established,—Yahweh hath sought out for him a man after his own heart, and Yahweh hath commanded him to be leader over his people, because thou hast not kept that which Yahweh commanded thee.
text.KJV: 13:14 But now thy kingdome shall not continue: the Lord hath sought him a man after his owne heart, and the Lord hath commanded him to bee captaine ouer his people, because thou hast not kept that which the Lord commanded thee.
text.DRV: 13:14 and went after the man of God, and found him sitting under a terebinth: and he said to him: Art thou the man of God that camest out of Juda? He answered: I am he.

text.NRSV: And Samuel left and went on his way from Gilgal. The rest of the people followed Saul to join the army; they went up from Gilgal toward Gibeah of Benjamin. Saul counted the people who were present with him, about six hundred men.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel arose, and ascended from Gilgal unto Gibeah of Benjamin. And Saul numbered the people who were found with him, about six hundred men.
text.KJV: 13:15 And Samuel arose, and gate him vp from Gilgal, vnto Gibeah of Beniamin, and Saul numbred the people that were present with him, about sixe hundred men.
text.DRV: 13:15 And he said to him: Come home with me, that thou mayst eat bread.

text.NRSV: Saul, his son Jonathan, and the people who were present with them stayed in Geba of Benjamin; but the Philistines encamped at Michmash.
text.Rotherham: Now, Saul, and Jonathan his son, and the people that were found with him, were abiding in Geba of Benjamin,—but, the Philistines, had encamped in Michmash.
text.KJV: 13:16 And Saul and Ionathan his sonne, and the people that were present with them, abode in Gibeah of Beniamin: but the Philistines encamped in Michmash.
text.DRV: 13:16 Who said: I cannot return, nor come with thee, neither will I eat bread, nor drink water in this place:

text.NRSV: And raiders came out of the camp of the Philistines in three companies; one company turned toward Ophrah, to the land of Shual,
text.Rotherham: And the spoilers came forth out of the camp of the Philistines, in three companies, the first company, turned unto the way of Ophrah, unto the land of Shual;
text.KJV: 13:17 ¶ And the spoilers came out of the campe of the Philistines, in three companies: one company turned vnto the way that leadeth to Ophrah, vnto the land of Shual.
text.DRV: 13:17 because our Lord spake to me in the word of our Lord, saying: Thou shalt not eat bread, and thou shalt not drink water there, nor return by the way thou wentest.

text.NRSV: another company turned toward Beth-horon, and another company turned toward the mountain that looks down upon the valley of Zeboim toward the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: and, the second company, turned unto the way of Beth-horon,—and, the third company, turned unto the way of the boundary that overlooketh the valley of Zeboim, towards the wilderness.
text.KJV: 13:18 And another company turned the way to Bethoron: and another companie turned to the way of the border, that looketh to the valley of Zeboim toward the wildernesse.
text.DRV: 13:18 Who said to him: I also am a Prophet like to thee: and an Angel hath spoken to me in the word of our Lord, saying: Bring him back with thee into thy house, that he may eat bread, and drink water. He deceived him,

text.NRSV: Now there was no smith to be found throughout all the land of Israel; for the Philistines said, “The Hebrews must not make swords or spears for themselves”;
text.Rotherham: Now, a smith, could not be found, throughout all the land of Israel—for the Philistines had said, Lest the Hebrews make sword or spear.
text.KJV: 13:19 ¶ Now there was no smith found thorowout all the land of Israel: for the Philistines said, Lest the Hebrewes make them swords or speares.
text.DRV: 13:19 and brought him back with him: he did eat therefore bread in his house, and drunk water.

text.NRSV: so all the Israelites went down to the Philistines to sharpen their plowshares, mattocks, axes, or sickles;
text.Rotherham: So all Israel went down to the Philistines,—to sharpen every man his share, and his mattock, and his axe, and his sickle.
text.KJV: 13:20 But all the Israelites went downe to the Philistines, to sharpen euery man his share and his coulter, and his axe, and his mattocke.
text.DRV: 13:20 And when they sat at the table, the word of our Lord came to the Prophet, that brought him back.

text.NRSV: The charge was two-thirds of a shekel for the plowshares and for the mattocks, and one-third of a shekel for sharpening the axes and for setting the goads.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit they had a file for the sickles, and the mattocks, and the three-pronged forks, and the axes,—and for setting the goads.
text.KJV: 13:21 Yet they had a file for the mattocks, and for the coulters, and for the forkes, and for the axes, and to sharpen the goads.
text.DRV: 13:21 And he cried out to the man of God, which came out of Juda, saying: Thus saith our Lord: Because thou hast not been obedient to the mouth of our Lord, and hast not kept the commandment which our Lord thy God commanded thee,

text.NRSV: So on the day of the battle neither sword nor spear was to be found in the possession of any of the people with Saul and Jonathan; but Saul and his son Jonathan had them.
text.Rotherham: Thus would it come to pass, in the day of battle, that there was found—neither sword nor spear, in the hand of any of the people, that were with Saul and Jonathan, but such were found belonging to Saul and to Jonathan his son.
text.KJV: 13:22 So it came to passe in the day of battell, that there was neither sword nor speare found in the hand of any of the people that were with Saul and Ionathan: but with Saul & with Ionathan his sonne was there found.
text.DRV: 13:22 and hast returned, and eaten bread, and drunk water in the place wherein he commanded thee that thou shouldest not eat bread, nor drink water, thy dead body shall not be brought into the sepulchre of thy fathers.

text.NRSV: Now a garrison of the Philistines had gone out to the pass of Michmash.
text.Rotherham: Now the garrison of the Philistines had come out to the pass of Michmash.
text.KJV: 13:23 And the garison of the Philistines went out to the passage of Michmash.
text.DRV: 13:23 And when he had eaten and drunk, he saddled his ass for the Prophet, whom he brought back.

text.NRSV: One day Jonathan son of Saul said to the young man who carried his armor, “Come, let us go over to the Philistine garrison on the other side.” But he did not tell his father.
text.Rotherham: And it came about, on a certain day, that Jonathan son of Saul said unto the young man bearing his armour: Come! and let us pass over unto the garrison of the Philistines, that is on the other side, yonder! but, to his father, he told it not.
text.KJV: 14:1 Now it came to passe vpon a day, that Ionathan the sonne of Saul said vnto the yong man that bare his armour, Come, and let vs goe ouer to the Philistines garison, that is on the other side: but hee told not his father.
text.DRV: 14:1 At that time Abia the son of Jeroboam was sick.

text.NRSV: Saul was staying in the outskirts of Gibeah under the pomegranate tree that is at Migron; the troops that were with him were about six hundred men,
text.Rotherham: Now, Saul, was tarrying in the uttermost part of Gibeah, under the pomegranate tree that is in Migron,—and, the people that were with him, were about six hundred men;
text.KJV: 14:2 And Saul taried in the vttermost part of Gibeah, vnder a Pomegranate tree, which is in Migron: and the people that were with him, were about sixe hundred men:
text.DRV: 14:2 And Jeroboam said to his wife: Arise, and change thy habit, that thou be not known to be the wife of Jeroboam, & go into Silo, where Ahias the Prophet is, which spake to me, that I should reign over this people.

text.NRSV: along with Ahijah son of Ahitub, Ichabod’s brother, son of Phinehas son of Eli, the priest of the Lord in Shiloh, carrying an ephod. Now the people did not know that Jonathan had gone.
text.Rotherham: and, Ahijah, son of Ahitub, brother of Ichabod, son of Phinehas, son of Eli, was priest of Yahweh in Shiloh, wearing an ephod,—and, the people, knew not that Jonathan had departed.
text.KJV: 14:3 And Ahiah the sonne of Ahitub, Ichabods brother, the sonne of Phinehas, the sonne of Eli, the Lords Priest in Shiloh, wearing an Ephod: and the people knew not that Ionathan was gone.
text.DRV: 14:3 Take also in thy hand ten loaves, & cracknels, & a vessel of honey, & go to him: for he will shew thee what shall happen to this child.

text.NRSV: In the pass, by which Jonathan tried to go over to the Philistine garrison, there was a rocky crag on one side and a rocky crag on the other; the name of the one was Bozez, and the name of the other Seneh.
text.Rotherham: And, between the passes by which Jonathan sought to cross over up to the garrison of the Philistines, there was a crag of rock on the one side, and a crag of rock on the other side; and, the name of the one, was Bozez, and the name of the other, Seneh.
text.KJV: 14:4 ¶ And betweene the passages, by which Ionathan sought to go ouer vnto the Philistines garison, there was a sharpe rocke on the one side, and a sharp rocke on the other side: and the name of the one was Bozez, and the name of the other Seneh.
text.DRV: 14:4 The wife of Jeroboam did as he had spoken: and rising up went into Silo, and came into the house of Ahias: but he could not see, because his eyes were dim for age.

text.NRSV: One crag rose on the north in front of Michmash, and the other on the south in front of Geba.
text.Rotherham: The one crag, was a pillar on the north, over against Michmash,—and, the other, on the south, over against Geba.
text.KJV: 14:5 The forefront of the one was situate Northward ouer against Michmash, and the other Southward ouer against Gibeah.
text.DRV: 14:5 And our Lord said to Ahias: Behold the wife of Jeroboam cometh in, to consult thee concerning her son that is sick: thus and thus shalt thou speak to her. When she therefore entered in, and dissembled to be that she was,

text.NRSV: Jonathan said to the young man who carried his armor, “Come, let us go over to the garrison of these uncircumcised; it may be that the Lord will act for us; for nothing can hinder the Lord from saving by many or by few.”
text.Rotherham: So Jonathan said unto the young man bearing his armour—Come! and let us cross over unto the garrison of these uncircumcised, peradventure, Yahweh may work by us,—for there is no restraint with Yahweh, to save by many, or by few.
text.KJV: 14:6 And Ionathan said to the young man that bare his armour, Come, and let vs goe ouer vnto the garison of these vncircumcised; it may be that the Lord will worke for vs: for there is no restraint to the Lord, to saue by many, or by few.
text.DRV: 14:6 Ahias heard the sound of her feet entering in at the door, and said: Come in Jeroboam's wife: Why doest thou feign thyself to be another woman? But I am sent to thee a heavy messenger.

text.NRSV: His armor-bearer said to him, “Do all that your mind inclines to. I am with you; as your mind is, so is mine.”
text.Rotherham: And his armour-bearer said to him, Do all that is in thy heart,—decide for thyself (lo! I am with thee!), according to thine own heart.
text.KJV: 14:7 And his armour bearer sayd vnto him, Doe all that is in thine heart: turne thee, behold, I am with thee, according to thy heart.
text.DRV: 14:7 Go, and tell Jeroboam: Thus saith our Lord the God of Israel: Because I have exalted thee out of the midst of the people, and made thee Prince over my people Israel,

text.NRSV: Then Jonathan said, “Now we will cross over to those men and will show ourselves to them.
text.Rotherham: Then said Jonathan, Lo! we, are crossing over unto the men,—and will discover ourselves unto them.
text.KJV: 14:8 Then said Ionathan, Behold, we will passe ouer vnto these men, and we will discouer our selues vnto them.
text.DRV: 14:8 and have rent the Kingdom of the house of David, and given it to thee, and thou hast not been as my servant David, who kept my commandments; and followed me in all his heart, doing that which was well liked in my sight:

text.NRSV: If they say to us, ‘Wait until we come to you,’ then we will stand still in our place, and we will not go up to them.
text.Rotherham: If, thus, they say unto us, Keep quiet until we get to you, then will we stand still where we are, and will not go up unto them;
text.KJV: 14:9 If they say thus vnto vs, Tary vntill we come to you: then wee will stand still in our place, and will not goe vp vnto them.
text.DRV: 14:9 but has wrought evil above all, that have been before thee, and hast made thee strange and molten gods, that thou mightest provoke me to anger, and hast rejected me behind thy back:

text.NRSV: But if they say, ‘Come up to us,’ then we will go up; for the Lord has given them into our hand. That will be the sign for us.”
text.Rotherham: but, if thus, they say, Come up unto us, then will we go up; for Yahweh hath delivered them into our hand,—This, then, is our sign.
text.KJV: 14:10 But if they say thus, Come vp vnto vs: then we will goe vp; for the Lord hath deliuered them into our hand: and this shall be a signe vnto vs.
text.DRV: 14:10 therefore behold I will bring in evils upon the house of Jeroboam, and will strike of Jeroboam him that pisseth to the wall, and the inclosed, and the vilest in Israel: and I will cleanse the remains of the house of Jeroboam, as dung is wont to be cleansed till all be pure.

text.NRSV: So both of them showed themselves to the garrison of the Philistines; and the Philistines said, “Look, Hebrews are coming out of the holes where they have hidden themselves.”
text.Rotherham: So they two discovered themselves unto the garrison of the Philistines,—and the Philistines said—Lo! Hebrews, coming forth out of the holes, wherein they had hidden themselves.
text.KJV: 14:11 And both of them discouered themselues vnto the garison of the Philistines: and the Philistines sayd, Behold, the Hebrewes come foorth out of the holes, where they had hid themselues.
text.DRV: 14:11 They that shall die of Jeroboam in the city, them the dogs shall eat: and they that shall die in the field, them the fowls of the air shall devour: because our Lord hath spoken.

text.NRSV: The men of the garrison hailed Jonathan and his armor-bearer, saying, “Come up to us, and we will show you something.” Jonathan said to his armor-bearer, “Come up after me; for the Lord has given them into the hand of Israel.”
text.Rotherham: And the men of the garrison responded to Jonathan and his armour-bearer, and said—Come up unto us, and we will let you know a thing. Then said Jonathan unto his armour-bearer—Come up after me, for Yahweh hath delivered them into the hand of Israel.
text.KJV: 14:12 And the men of the garison answered Ionathan and his armour bearer, and said, Come vp to vs, and wee will shew you a thing. And Ionathan said vnto his armour bearer, Come vp after me; for the Lord hath deliuered them into the hand of Israel.
text.DRV: 14:12 Thou therefore arise, and go into thy house: and in the very entrance of thy feet into the city, the child shall die,

text.NRSV: Then Jonathan climbed up on his hands and feet, with his armor-bearer following after him. The Philistines fell before Jonathan, and his armor-bearer, coming after him, killed them.
text.Rotherham: So Jonathan went up on his hands and on his feet, and, his armour-bearer, followed him,—and they fell before Jonathan, and, his armour-bearer, was slaying after him.
text.KJV: 14:13 And Ionathan climed vp vpon his hands, and vpon his feete, and his armour bearer after him: and they fell before Ionathan; and his armour bearer slew after him.
text.DRV: 14:13 & all Israel shall mourn for him, and shall bury him: for this only of Jeroboam shall be brought into the sepulchre, because upon him hath been found a good word from our Lord the God of Israel, in the house of Jeroboam.

text.NRSV: In that first slaughter Jonathan and his armor-bearer killed about twenty men within an area about half a furrow long in an acre of land.
text.Rotherham: And the first smiting wherewith Jonathan and his armour-bearer smote, was about twenty men,—within, as it were, half a furrow’s length of a yoke of land.
text.KJV: 14:14 And that first slaughter which Ionathan and his armour-bearer made, was about twentie men, within as it were an halfe acre of land, which a yoke of oxen might plow.
text.DRV: 14:14 And our Lord will appoint to himself a king over Israel, that shall strike the house of Jeroboam in this day, and in this time:

text.NRSV: There was a panic in the camp, in the field, and among all the people; the garrison and even the raiders trembled; the earth quaked; and it became a very great panic.
text.Rotherham: And there came to be a trembling in the camp, in the field, and among all the people, the outposts and the spoilers, they too, trembled,—and the earth quaked, so it became a preternatural trembling.
text.KJV: 14:15 And there was trembling in the hoste, in the field, and among all the people: the garison and the spoilers, they also trembled, and the earth quaked: so it was a very great trembling.
text.DRV: 14:15 and our Lord the God of Israel shall strike it, as a reed is wont to be moved in the water: and he shall pluck out Israel from this good country, which he gave to their fathers, and shall scatter them over the River: because they have made to themselves groves, to provoke our Lord.

text.NRSV: Saul’s sentinels in Gibeah of Benjamin were watching as the multitude was surging back and forth.
text.Rotherham: And the scouts of Saul in Gibeah of Benjamin looked, and lo! the camp, melted away, hither and thither.
text.KJV: 14:16 And the watchmen of Saul in Gibeah of Beniamin looked: and behold, the multitude melted away, and they went on beating downe one another.
text.DRV: 14:16 And our Lord shall deliver Israel for the sins of Jeroboam, who hath sinned, and made Israel to sin.

text.NRSV: Then Saul said to the troops that were with him, “Call the roll and see who has gone from us.” When they had called the roll, Jonathan and his armor-bearer were not there.
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul unto the people who were with him: Number, I pray you, and see who hath departed from us. So they numbered; and lo! Jonathan and his armour-bearer were missing.
text.KJV: 14:17 Then said Saul vnto the people that were with him, Number now, and see who is gone from vs. And when they had numbred, behold, Ionathan and his armour bearer were not there.
text.DRV: 14:17 The wife therefore of Jeroboam arose, and departed, and came into Thersa: and when she entered the threshold of the house, the child died,

text.NRSV: Saul said to Ahijah, “Bring the ark of God here.” For at that time the ark of God went with the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul unto Ahijah, Bring near the ark of God; for the ark of God was, on that day, in the midst of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 14:18 And Saul said vnto Ahiah, Bring hither the Arke of God: (for the Arke of God was at that time with the children of Israel.)
text.DRV: 14:18 and they buried him. And all Israel mourned for him according to the word of our Lord, which he spake in the hand of his servant Ahias the Prophet.

text.NRSV: While Saul was talking to the priest, the tumult in the camp of the Philistines increased more and more; and Saul said to the priest, “Withdraw your hand.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as soon as Saul had spoken unto the priest, that, the tumultuous noise that was in the camp of the Philistines, went on and on, increasing. Then said Saul unto the priest,—Withdraw thy hand.
text.KJV: 14:19 ¶ And it came to passe while Saul talked vnto the Priest, that the noise that was in the hoste of the Philistines went on, and increased: And Saul said vnto the Priest, Withdraw thine hand.
text.DRV: 14:19 But the rest of the words of Jeroboam, how he fought, and how he reigned, behold they are written in the Book of the words of the days of the kings of Israel.

text.NRSV: Then Saul and all the people who were with him rallied and went into the battle; and every sword was against the other, so that there was very great confusion.
text.Rotherham: And Saul and all the people that were with him gathered themselves together, and came as far as the host,—and lo! the sword of every man was against his fellow, an exceeding great confusion.
text.KJV: 14:20 And Saul and all the people that were with him assembled themselues, and they came to the battel, and behold, euery mans sword was against his fellow, and there was a very great discomfiture.
text.DRV: 14:20 And the days, that Jeroboam reigned, are two and twenty years: and he slept with his fathers: and Nadab his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: Now the Hebrews who previously had been with the Philistines and had gone up with them into the camp turned and joined the Israelites who were with Saul and Jonathan.
text.Rotherham: And, the Hebrews who had aforetime belonged to the Philistines, who had come up with them in the host, even they, turned round so as to be with Israel who were with Saul and Jonathan.
text.KJV: 14:21 Moreouer, the Hebrewes that were with the Philistines before that time, which went vp with them into the campe from the countrey round about; euen they also turned to be with the Israelites, that were with Saul and Ionathan.
text.DRV: 14:21 Moreover Roboam the son of Salomon reigned in Juda: one and forty years old was Roboam when he began to reign: seventeen years reigned he in Jerusalem the city, which our Lord chose to put his name there, of all the tribes of Israel. And his mother's name was Naama an Ammonite.

text.NRSV: Likewise, when all the Israelites who had gone into hiding in the hill country of Ephraim heard that the Philistines were fleeing, they too followed closely after them in the battle.
text.Rotherham: And, all the men of Israel who had hidden themselves throughout the hill country of Ephraim, heard that the Philistines had fled, and, they also, followed hard after them in the battle.
text.KJV: 14:22 Likewise all the men of Israel, which had hid themselues in mount Ephraim, when they heard that the Philistines fled, euen they also followed hard after them in the battell.
text.DRV: 14:22 And Judas did evil before our Lord, and provoked him above all things, that their fathers had done, in their sins which they sinned.

text.NRSV: So the Lord gave Israel the victory that day. The battle passed beyond Beth-aven, and the troops with Saul numbered altogether about ten thousand men. The battle spread out over the hill country of Ephraim.
text.Rotherham: Thus did Yahweh, on that day, save Israel,—and, the battle, passed over by Beth-aven.
text.KJV: 14:23 So the Lord saued Israel that day: and the battell passed ouer vnto Beth-auen.
text.DRV: 14:23 For they also built them altars, & statues, & groves upon every high hill, and under every tree full of green leaves:

text.NRSV: Now Saul committed a very rash act on that day. He had laid an oath on the troops, saying, “Cursed be anyone who eats food before it is evening and I have been avenged on my enemies.” So none of the troops tasted food.
text.Rotherham: Now, the men of Israel, were tired out on that day,—yet had Saul bound the people by an oath, saying—Cursed, be the man that eateth food until the evening, and I be avenged upon mine enemies. So none of the people had tasted food.
text.KJV: 14:24 ¶ And the men of Israel were distressed that day; for Saul had adiured the people, saying, Cursed bee the man that eateth any foode vntill euening, that I may be auenged on mine enemies: so none of the people tasted any food.
text.DRV: 14:24 yea and effeminates were in the land, and they did all the abominations of the gentiles, which our Lord destroyed before the face of the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: All the troops came upon a honeycomb; and there was honey on the ground.
text.Rotherham: And, all the land, had entered into the forest,—and there was honey upon the face of the ground.
text.KJV: 14:25 And all they of the land came to a wood, and there was honie vpon the ground.
text.DRV: 14:25 And in the fifth year of the reign of Roboam, Sesac the king of Ægypt came up into Jerusalem,

text.NRSV: When the troops came upon the honeycomb, the honey was flowing; but they did not put their hands to their mouths, for they feared the oath.
text.Rotherham: So the people came into the forest, and lo! there were streams of honey,—but no man reached his hand to his mouth, because the people revered the oath.
text.KJV: 14:26 And when the people were come into the wood, behold, the honie dropped, but no man put his hand to his mouth: for the people feared the oath.
text.DRV: 14:26 and took the treasures of the house of our Lord, and the kings' treasures, and all things he spoiled: the shields also of gold, which Salomon had made:

text.NRSV: But Jonathan had not heard his father charge the troops with the oath; so he extended the staff that was in his hand, and dipped the tip of it in the honeycomb, and put his hand to his mouth; and his eyes brightened.
text.Rotherham: But, Jonathan, heard not his father putting the people on oath, so he reached forth the end of the staff that was in his hand, and dipped it in the honey-copse,—and brought back his hand to his mouth, and his eyes were brightened.
text.KJV: 14:27 But Ionathan heard not when his father charged the people with the oath; wherefore he put foorth the ende of the rodde that was in his hand, and dipt it in an hony combe, and put his hand to his mouth, and his eyes were enlightened.
text.DRV: 14:27 for the which Roboam made brazen shields, and delivered them into the hand of the captains of shield-bearers, and of them that kept watch before the door of the king's house.

text.NRSV: Then one of the soldiers said, “Your father strictly charged the troops with an oath, saying, ‘Cursed be anyone who eats food this day.’ And so the troops are faint.”
text.Rotherham: Then responded one from among the people, and said—With an oath, did thy father charge the people, saying—Cursed, be the man that eateth food to-day! And so the people were faint.
text.KJV: 14:28 Then answered one of the people, and said, Thy father straitly charged the people with an oath, saying, Cursed be the man that eateth any food this day. And the people were faint.
text.DRV: 14:28 And when the king went into the house of our Lord, they that had the office to go before, carried them: and afterward they recarried them to the armoury of the shield-bearers.

text.NRSV: Then Jonathan said, “My father has troubled the land; see how my eyes have brightened because I tasted a little of this honey.
text.Rotherham: Then said Jonathan: My father hath afflicted the land,—See, I pray you, how my own eyes have been brightened, because I tasted a little of this honey.
text.KJV: 14:29 Then said Ionathan, My father hath troubled the land: see, I pray you, how mine eyes haue beene enlightened, because I tasted a little of this honie:
text.DRV: 14:29 And the rest of the words of Roboam, and all that he did, behold they are written in the Book of the words of the days of the kings of Juda.

text.NRSV: How much better if today the troops had been allowed to eat freely of the spoil taken from their enemies; for the slaughter among the Philistines has not been great.”
text.Rotherham: How much more if haply the people had, eaten freely, to-day of the spoil of their enemies, which they found? for, now, would not the smiting of the Philistines have been, mighty?
text.KJV: 14:30 How much more, if haply the people had eaten freely to day of the spoile of their enemies which they found? for had there not beene now a much greater slaughter among the Philistines?
text.DRV: 14:30 And there was war between Roboam and Jeroboam always.

text.NRSV: They struck down the Philistines that day from Michmash to Aijalon. And the troops were very faint;
text.Rotherham: Howbeit they smote the Philistines on that day from Michmash to Aijalon,—but the people became exceeding faint.
text.KJV: 14:31 And they smote the Philistines that day from Michmash to Aiialon: and the people were very faint.
text.DRV: 14:31 And Roboam slept with his fathers, and was buried with them in the city of David: and his mother's name was Naama an Ammonite: and Abias his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: so the troops flew upon the spoil, and took sheep and oxen and calves, and slaughtered them on the ground; and the troops ate them with the blood.
text.Rotherham: And the people darted greedily upon the spoil, and took sheep, and oxen, and calves, and felled them to the ground,—and the people did eat, with the blood.
text.KJV: 14:32 And the people flewe vpon the spoile, and tooke sheepe, and oxen, and calues, and slew them on the ground, and the people did eate them with the blood.
text.DRV: 14:32 and being turned to the prey took sheep, and oxen, and calves, and slew them on the ground: and the people did eat with blood.

text.NRSV: Then it was reported to Saul, “Look, the troops are sinning against the Lord by eating with the blood.” And he said, “You have dealt treacherously; roll a large stone before me here.”
text.Rotherham: And they told Saul, saying, Lo! the people, are sinning against Yahweh, by eating with the blood. And he said—Ye have dealt treacherously, roll unto me, here, a great stone.
text.KJV: 14:33 ¶ Then they tolde Saul, saying, Behold, the people sinne against the Lord, in that they eate with the blood. And he said, Yee haue transgressed: roule a great stone vnto mee this day.
text.DRV: 14:33 And they told Saul saying that the people had sinned to our Lord, eating with blood. Who said: You have transgressed: Roll to me even now a great stone.

text.NRSV: Saul said, “Disperse yourselves among the troops, and say to them, ‘Let each man bring his ox or his sheep, and slaughter them here, and eat; and do not sin against the Lord by eating with the blood.’” So every one of the troops brought his ox with him that night, and slaughtered it there.
text.Rotherham: And Saul said—Disperse yourselves among the people, and say unto them—Bring near unto me every man his ox, and every man his lamb, and slay them here, and then eat, so shall ye not sin against Yahweh, by eating with the blood. And all the people brought near—every man that which was in his hand, that night, and they slew them there.
text.KJV: 14:34 And Saul said, Disperse your selues among the people, and say vnto them, Bring me hither euery man his oxe, and euery man his sheepe, and slay them here, and eat, and sinne not against the Lord in eating with the blood. And all the people brought euery man his oxe with him that night, and slew them there.
text.DRV: 14:34 And Saul said: Disperse yourselves among the common people, and tell them that every man bring me his oxe and ram, and kill ye them upon this same, and eat, and you shall not sin to our Lord eating with blood. All the people therefore brought every man his oxe in his hand until night: and slew them there.

text.NRSV: And Saul built an altar to the Lord; it was the first altar that he built to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And Saul built an altar unto Yahweh,—the same, was the first altar that he built unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 14:35 And Saul built an altar vnto the Lord: the same was the first altar that he built vnto the Lord.
text.DRV: 14:35 And Saul built an altar to our Lord; and then first did he begin to build an altar to our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Saul said, “Let us go down after the Philistines by night and despoil them until the morning light; let us not leave one of them.” They said, “Do whatever seems good to you.” But the priest said, “Let us draw near to God here.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul—Let us go down after the Philistines by night, and make of them a prey until the morning light, and let us not leave of them, a man. And they said, All that is good in thine eyes, do! Then said the priest, Let us draw near hither unto God.
text.KJV: 14:36 ¶ And Saul saide, Let vs goe downe after the Philistines by night, and spoile them vntil the morning light, and let vs not leaue a man of them. And they said, Do whatsoeuer seemeth good vnto thee. Then said the priest, Let vs draw neere hither vnto God.
text.DRV: 14:36 And Saul said: Let us fall upon the Philisthiims by night, and let us spoil them till it wax light in the morning, neither let us leave a man of them. And the people said: Do all that seemeth good in thine eyes. And the Priest said: Let us approach hither to God.

text.NRSV: So Saul inquired of God, “Shall I go down after the Philistines? Will you give them into the hand of Israel?” But he did not answer him that day.
text.Rotherham: So Saul asked of God, Shall I go down after the Philistines? wilt thou deliver them into the hand of Israel? But he answered him not, that day.
text.KJV: 14:37 And Saul asked counsell of God, Shall I goe downe after the Philistines? Wilt thou deliuer them into the hand of Israel? But he answered him not that day.
text.DRV: 14:37 And Saul consulted our Lord: Shall I pursue the Philistiims? Wilt thou deliver them into the hands of Israel? And he answered him not in that day.

text.NRSV: Saul said, “Come here, all you leaders of the people; and let us find out how this sin has arisen today.
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul, Come near hither, all ye chiefs of the people,—and get to know and see, wherein, hath been this sin, to-day.
text.KJV: 14:38 And Saul said, Draw yee neere hither all the chiefe of the people: and know and see, wherein this sinne hath beene this day.
text.DRV: 14:38 And Saul said: Bring hither all the corners of the people: and know, and see by whom this sin hath chanced today.

text.NRSV: For as the Lord lives who saves Israel, even if it is in my son Jonathan, he shall surely die!” But there was no one among all the people who answered him.
text.Rotherham: For, by the life of Yahweh, who saveth Israel, though it be in Jonathan my son, yet shall he, die. But there was none ready to answer him, of all the people.
text.KJV: 14:39 For as the Lord liueth, which saueth Israel, though it bee in Ionathan my sonne, he shall surely die: But there was not a man among all the people that answered him.
text.DRV: 14:39 Our Lord the Savior of Israel liveth, that if it were done by Jonathas my son, he shall die without revoking. Whereunto none of the people gainsaid him.

text.NRSV: He said to all Israel, “You shall be on one side, and I and Jonathan my son will be on the other side.” The people said to Saul, “Do what seems good to you.”
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto all Israel—Ye, shall be on one side, and, I and Jonathan my son, will be on the other side. And the people said unto Saul, What is good in thine eyes, do!
text.KJV: 14:40 Then said he vnto all Israel, Be ye on one side, and I, and Ionathan my sonne will be on the other side. And the people said vnto Saul, Doe what seemeth good vnto thee.
text.DRV: 14:40 And he said to all Israel: Be you separated into one side, and I with Jonathas my son will be on the other side. And the people answered Saul: Do what seemeth good in thine eyes.

text.NRSV: Then Saul said, “O Lord God of Israel, why have you not answered your servant today? If this guilt is in me or in my son Jonathan, O Lord God of Israel, give Urim; but if this guilt is in your people Israel, give Thummim.” And Jonathan and Saul were selected, but the people were cleared.
text.Rotherham: And Saul said unto Yahweh—O God of Israel! now set forth the truth. Then were, Jonathan and Saul, taken, and, the people, escaped.
text.KJV: 14:41 Therefore Saul saide vnto the Lord God of Israel, Giue a perfect lot. And Saul and Ionathan were taken: but the people escaped.
text.DRV: 14:41 And Saul said to our Lord: Lord God of Israel, give a sign: and Jonathas was caught and Saul, and the people went forth.

text.NRSV: Then Saul said, “Cast the lot between me and my son Jonathan.” And Jonathan was selected.
text.Rotherham: And Saul said, Cast lots between me, and Jonathan my son. Then was, Jonathan, taken.
text.KJV: 14:42 And Saul said, Cast lots betweene me and Ionathan my sonne. And Ionathan was taken.
text.DRV: 14:42 And Saul said: Cast ye lot between me, and Jonathas my son. And Jonathas was taken.

text.NRSV: Saul said to Jonathan, “Tell me what you have done.” Jonathan told him, “I tasted a little honey with the tip of the staff that was in my hand; here I am, I will die.”
text.Rotherham: And Saul said unto Jonathan, Come tell me, what thou hast done. So Jonathan told him, and said, I, just tasted, with the end of the staff that was in my hand, a little honey, here I am—I must die!
text.KJV: 14:43 Then Saul said to Ionathan, Tell me what thou hast done. And Ionathan tolde him, and said, I did but taste a litle hony with the end of the rodde that was in mine hand, and loe, I must die.
text.DRV: 14:43 And Saul said to Jonathas: Tell me what thou hast done. And Jonathas told him, and said: Tasting I tasted in the tip of the rod which was in mine hand a little honey, & behold I die.

text.NRSV: Saul said, “God do so to me and more also; you shall surely die, Jonathan!”
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul: So, may God do, and, so, may he add, surely thou must, die, Jonathan!
text.KJV: 14:44 And Saul answered, God do so, and more also: for thou shalt surely die, Ionathan.
text.DRV: 14:44 And Saul said: These things do God to me, & these things add he, that dying thou shalt die Jonathas.

text.NRSV: Then the people said to Saul, “Shall Jonathan die, who has accomplished this great victory in Israel? Far from it! As the Lord lives, not one hair of his head shall fall to the ground; for he has worked with God today.” So the people ransomed Jonathan, and he did not die.
text.Rotherham: But the people said unto Saul—Shall, Jonathan, die, who hath wrought this great salvation in Israel? Far be it! By the life of Yahweh, there shall not fall a hair of his head to the ground, for, with God, hath he wrought this day. So the people delivered Jonathan, that he died not.
text.KJV: 14:45 And the people said vnto Saul, Shall Ionathan die, who hath wrought this great saluation in Israel? God forbid: as the Lord liueth, there shal not one haire of his head fall to the ground: for hee hath wrought with God this day. So the people rescued Ionathan, that hee died not.
text.DRV: 14:45 And the people said to Saul: Shall Jonathas then die, which hath made this great salvation in Israel? This is unlawful: our Lord liveth, if there shall fall a hair from his head upon the ground, because with God hath he wrought today. The people therefore delivered Jonathas, that he should not die.

text.NRSV: Then Saul withdrew from pursuing the Philistines; and the Philistines went to their own place.
text.Rotherham: Then Saul went up from following the Philistines,—and, the Philistines, departed unto their own place.
text.KJV: 14:46 Then Saul went vp from following the Philistines: and the Philistines went to their owne place.
text.DRV: 14:46 And Saul retired, neither did he pursue the Philistiims: moreover the Philistiims departed into their places.

text.NRSV: When Saul had taken the kingship over Israel, he fought against all his enemies on every side—against Moab, against the Ammonites, against Edom, against the kings of Zobah, and against the Philistines; wherever he turned he was victorious.
text.Rotherham: And, Saul, took possession of the kingdom over Israel,—and made war round about against all his enemies—against Moab, and against the sons of Ammon, and against Edom, and against the kings of Zobah, and against the Philistines, and, whomsoever he turned against, he was victorious.
text.KJV: 14:47 ¶ So Saul tooke the kingdom ouer Israel, and fought against all his enemies on euery side, against Moab, and against the children of Ammon, and against Edom, and against the kings of Zobah, and against the Philistines: and whithersoeuer hee turned himselfe, he vexed them.
text.DRV: 14:47 And Saul, his kingdom being established over Israel, fought round about against all his enemies, against Moab, & the children of Ammon, and Edom, and the kings of Soba, and the Philisthians: and whither soever he turned himself, he overcame.
